dateUncertaintyNote: The date marks early medieval European church/monastic bell-casting practice; bell founding itself is earlier in Bronze Age East Asia.

sources.title: Origin of bells

sources.title: Bell
sources.source_locator: Britannica identifies bell founding as the casting of bells from molten metal and associates the earliest bell founding with the Bronze Age, anchoring the broader craft scope.

name: Fortified Water Gates

dependencyEdges.note: Fortified water gates were gates in defensive wall circuits leading to river fords, so wall-and-gate fortification is the enabling context rather than beacon signaling.

sources.title: Fortification of the Peninsula
sources.source_locator: Durham World Heritage Site says masonry walls were built around the peninsula between 1099 and 1128, strengthened with towers, and included gates leading to fords across the river, including Water Gate.

sources.title: Water Gate, City of Durham

sources.title: Fortification of the Peninsula

sources.title: Political Culture and Urban Space in Early Tudor London
sources.source_locator: pp. 194-197: describes London household ward watches in the 1100s, regular summer ward watches from the 1340s, night watch duties rotating among householders, and civic regulation of streets, houses, fire risks, and ward enforcement.

sources.title: The Statute of Winchester
sources.source_locator: The SAGE excerpt dates the Statute of Winchester to 1285, identifies watchmen assisting constables, hue-and-cry obligations, citizen arrest, and town gates closed between sunset and sunrise.

sources.title: Political Culture and Urban Space in Early Tudor London
sources.source_locator: pp. 194-197: covers London ward watches, rotating household night-watch duties, and municipal regulation of streets, houses, fire risks, and ward enforcement.

scopeNote: Covers organized nighttime civic watch duties for gates, patrols, hue-and-cry, curfew, and fire-risk enforcement. It is narrower than all town policing and does not claim mechanical clock towers, public street lighting, or professional police forces as prerequisites.
